text: The surgeon initiates the procedure using a middle to lateral approach, with the robotic system positioned on the left side of the patient.
The surgeon sets the target for a single docking procedure using the DAVINCI XI robotic system, positioned on the left side of the patient.
The surgeon dissects the inferior mesenteric artery approximately 1.5 centimeters from the aorta, using robotic instruments positioned through ports placed diagonally across the abdomen.
The surgeon clips the targeted structure with a Hem-o-lok clip and then sections it using monopolar scissors.
The surgeon dissects the inferior mesenteric vein, clips it with a surgical clip, and sections it using scissors.
The surgeon continues the dissection using a middle to lateral approach, carefully preserving the Girota fascia as the tissue layers are separated.
The surgeon identifies the left ureter, visible within the surgical field, as dissection progresses in the colorectal procedure.
The surgeon continues the dissection until the lower border of the pancreas is visualized, aiding in the mobilization of the splenic flexure.
A sterile gauze is placed in the area before dissecting the gastrocolic ligament to help identify the correct dissection plane as the surgeon begins the lateral to medial mobilization.
The surgeon completes the mobilization of the left colon by dissecting the tall fascia using monopolar scissors and a vessel sealer, ensuring separation of the colon from surrounding structures.

The surgeon begins the total mesorectal excision by dissecting the posterior plane, followed by the right and left sides, and finally the anterior plane, progressing until reaching the elevator honey.
The surgeon adjusts the thirty-degree camera from a bottom-to-top orientation at the remote console to facilitate the dissection of the last centimeters of the posterior mesorectum.
The surgeon adjusts the view to enhance visualization of the anorectal angle, preparing for further dissection in this area.
The surgeon completes the mesorectal dissection towards the levator ani and performs an ileostomy at the designated site, finalizing the procedure with an anal approach.

The surgeon uses a long star retractor to expose the rectal stump, then performs a transaction using monopolar cautery, five millimeters above the dentate line, employing a sphincter-preserving technique.
The robotic dissection plane is accessed from bottom to top, and the surgical specimen is extracted through the anus.
The surgeon prepares the bulbar stump for anastomosis construction, ensuring the lymphadenectomy is respected.
The surgeon verifies the vascularization of the colonic stumps using induced fluorescence angiography before dividing the colon for the end-to-end anastomosis.
The surgeon performs an attention-free coloanal anastomosis using twelve single stitches of Vicryl-TRIO, carefully suturing the colon to the anal canal.
